label: Capital expenditure phasing and benefit ramp-up profiles (all jurisdictions)
description: S-curve (bell-shaped) spending profiles for capital cost phasing. These are mathematical shapes, not jurisdiction-specific. The same profiles apply to UK, US, EU, and AU CBAs. Project-specific phasing from cost consultants always takes precedence.

benefit_ramp_up.description: Benefit ramp-up profiles for operational phase. First operational year = proportion of steady-state benefit.

source.publication: Standard infrastructure project management practice. UK Green Book, US OMB A-94, AACE International, and World Bank all use similar bell-curve phasing assumptions.

caveats: These are generic profiles. Project-specific phasing from cost consultants is always preferred.
Large infrastructure projects often have more complex phasing with site preparation, procurement, and commissioning phases.
The same S-curve shapes are used globally. Only project-specific adjustments (e.g., procurement delays, seasonal construction) would differ by jurisdiction.
